data.title: Thyssenkrupp ends JV talks with EP Group, opening path for rival suitor
data.description:
Thyssenkrupp (OTCPK:TYEKF) (OTCPK:TKAMY) -4% in Frankfurt trading Thursday after saying it ended talks with EP Group on a possible 50-50 joint venture for Steel Europe, clearing the way for a rival offer from India's Jindal Steel.

The German industrial company said EP Group, owned by Czech entrepreneur Daniel Kretinsky, has withdrawn from further talks and that it would acquire the 20% of Thyssenkrupp Steel Europe (OTCPK:TYEKF) (OTCPK:TKAMY) sold to EPG for the purchase price it paid.

EP Group took a 20% position in Thyssenkrupp Steel Europe (OTCPK:TYEKF) (OTCPK:TKAMY) early last year, with an aim to raise its stake to 50%, but talks subsequently dragged on, and EPG said it agreed to return its 20% stake.

The move creates an opportunity for Thyssenkrupp (OTCPK:TYEKF) (OTCPK:TKAMY) to intensify talks with Jindal Steel, which submitted an indicative bid last month for all of Thyssenkrupp Steel Europe.

Thyssenkrupp (OTCPK:TYEKF) (OTCPK:TKAMY) has tried unsuccessfully a number of times to sell its steel business in an attempt to reduce complexity, boost competitiveness and increase profitability.

data.title: Tesla turns the quarter with 'bounceback' Q3 deliveries - Wedbush
data.description:
Tesla’s (NASDAQ:TSLA) third quarter deliveries quieted some of the critical chatter surrounding the company, ever since the board proposed a $1 trillion payday to its CEO if ambitious goals are met.

The company’s delivery numbers beat expectations, and even set a quarterly record as buyers rushed to take advantage of the now-expired $7,500 tax credit on electric vehicles.

But the momentum was not just in the U.S., but in China as well where the company continues to face intense competition from lower-priced options from Chinese rivals BYD (OTCPK:BYDDY) (OTCPK:BYDDF), NIO (NIO), and Xpeng (XPEV).

“China remains a positive point for Tesla,” Wedbush’s Dan Ives said in the wake of Q3 figures, with the recently updated Model Y spurring continued demand in the region while the new six-seat Model Y L has seen “a significant rise in registrations to ~20% of its total TSLA China registrations.” All of which suggest that China could add “incremental growth” to this key region moving forward.

“While EV demand is expected to fall with the EV tax credit expiration, this was a great bounceback quarter for Tesla to lay the groundwork for deliveries moving forward,” Ives says tempering his outsized optimism by acknowledging that there is still work to do to gain further ground from a delivery perspective.

Despite strong deliveries, all eyes remain on the company’s autonomous ambitions, and Ives remains confident Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) can reach a $2 trillion market cap in early 2026 and even $3 trillion by the end of 2026.

“We believe the march to an AI-driven valuation for Tesla over the next 6–9 months has now begun,” confident that with Musk at the helm as a “wartime CEO” and initiatives under a Trump White House to remain ahead of the Chinese in the AI race, Tesla’s (NASDAQ:TSLA) AI and autonomous opportunity alone is worth at least $1 trillion.

data.title: Analysts split on Corteva spin: J.P. Morgan turns bullish, Citi cautious, UBS stays optimistic
data.description:
Wall Street is sharply divided over Corteva’s plan to split into two standalone companies, with analysts disagreeing on whether the move unlocks long-term value or amplifies near-term risks.

JPMorgan: Upgrade to Overweight

J.P. Morgan’s Jeffrey Zekauskas on Thursday upgraded Corteva to Overweight from Neutral, arguing that the market reaction (a 10% sell-off following the announcement) was excessive. He views the split as a way to highlight the faster-growing seed business while allowing the crop chemicals arm to re-rate closer to peers. Zekauskas sees the seed spin-off valued at about 13.5 times earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization, with the chemicals unit trading at six times on 2025 estimates, a deep discount to rival FMC’s (FMC) 7.5 to eight times multiple. His $70 price target remains unchanged, with the analyst calling current valuations “an undervaluation rather than a destruction of value”

Citi: Downgrade to Neutral

Citi’s Patrick Cunningham took the opposite tack, cutting Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) to Neutral from Buy despite the stock’s pullback. Cunningham said the split “adds more questions than answers” at a time when farmer margins are already pressured by subdued grain prices and input cost volatility. He warned that dis-synergies, execution risks, and a challenging 2026 setup could weigh on earnings. Citi trimmed its price target to $67 from $83, flagging the potential for downward estimate revisions in both FY26 and FY27 .

UBS: Maintains Buy

UBS analysts led by Joshua Spector maintained a Buy rating with a $91 price target, framing the stock’s decline as an overreaction. While acknowledging investor concerns about crop chemical headwinds, timing, and potential liabilities, UBS argued that Corteva’s (NYSE:CTVA) valuation at around $62 a share already reflects highly conservative assumptions. The bank noted that seeds have historically grown ebitda at an approximately 11% CAGR, outpacing specialty chemical peers, and could eventually trade at premium multiples. UBS sees up to 30% upside in a bullish re-rating scenario, with seeds revalued like high-quality specialty chemical firms

Divided opinion

The divergent calls highlight the uncertainty surrounding Corteva’s (CTVA) transformation. To some, the spin-off clarifies the growth story by separating a higher-margin seed business from a legacy-burdened chemicals arm. To others, it heightens execution risk in an already volatile agricultural market.

Investors now face a choice between JPMorgan’s undervaluation thesis, Citi’s warning of near-term downside, and UBS’s bet on a longer-term re-rating, all as Corteva (CTVA) prepares to reshape itself ahead of 2026.

data.title: European indexes touch multi-month highs, STOXX touches fresh record
data.description:
Update: The post reflects European markets closing indices.

London (UKX) -0.20% to 9,428, pausing after hitting a record high in the prior session.

Germany (DAX:IND) +1.28% to 24,423.

France (CAC:IND) +1.13% to 8,057, its highest level since late March, driven by strength in chipmakers. 

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) turned 0.53% higher to 567.6, extending the previous session’s rally that drove both benchmarks to fresh record closes. 

In the bond market, the yield on the US 10-year Treasury was unchanged at 4.10%.

Germany's 10-year yield was unchanged at 2.70%.

UK's 10-year yield was down 1 basis point at 4.71%. 

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NYSEARCA:EWI), (NYSEARCA:EWQ), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR), (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB), (NYSEARCA:GREK)

Seeking Alpha Editor Khushi Singh contributed to this article.

data.title: Wall Street hunts for direction on day two of the government shutdown
data.description:
Wall Street looks for direction after an early pop on Thursday that sent both the tech focused Nasdaq Composite and benchmark S&P 500 to new intraday record highs as the government shutdown hit a second day.

For the trading session the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was up by 0.2% and the S&P 500 was near even. At the same time, the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was lower by 0.1%. 

From a sector-by-sector vantage point, nine of the 11 S&P segments were in negative trading territory as Real Estate struggled the most so far. At the other end of the spectrum, Materials has been the leader on Thursday. 

U.S. Treasury yields inched higher as the longer-end U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) moved up 1 basis point to 4.11% and the shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) pushed up 2 basis points to 3.56%.

Due to the closure, market participants will be without the usual jobless claims figures. On prediction market Kalshi, the odds of the length of the shutdown ticked up to 11.5 days.

Focus was on the September Challenger job cuts report, which was reported earlier than its scheduled time of 0730 ET. A Challenger spokeswoman told Seeking Alpha that it was a mistake in scheduling and not intentional.

The report showed job cuts fell 37% in September. However, YTD cuts were now the highest since 2020, and hiring the lowest since 2009.

Regarding day two of the government shutdown, Glen Smith, chief investment officer, GDS Wealth Management said: "Government shutdowns are historically not a major market moving event. The most recent government shutdown in 2018-2019 happened to have coincided with the late 2018 selloff, which started to reverse in early 2019 when the Federal Reserve at the time hinted that it was open to cutting rates, and that sentiment shift in early 2019 took place while the government was shutdown."

data.title: Corteva upgraded by J.P. Morgan after negative market reaction to split
data.description:
J.P. Morgan on Thursday upgraded Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) to Overweight from a previous investment rating of Neutral, arguing that investor concerns over the company’s planned split into two businesses are overdone and that the market has created an opportunity to buy undervalued shares.

Corteva (NYSE:CTVA) this week announced it will divide into two standalone companies in the second half of 2026: a pure-play seed business (“Spinco”) and a crop chemicals company (“New Corteva”).

The separation will leave New Corteva with the company’s legacy PFAS litigation liabilities and DuPont (DD) pension obligations, while Spinco will remain unburdened by those costs. Importantly, J.P. Morgan noted, New Corteva will have no claim on Spinco’s future cash flows for PFAS responsibilities as long as its earnings remain above a set threshold for earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization.

Analyst Jeffrey Zekauskas wrote that the rationale is to unlock faster growth in the higher-quality seed business, while allowing each company to focus on its strengths. Spinco, with gross margins above 50% and lower leverage, should benefit from “trading multiple expansion,” while New Corteva will continue to generate cash as a dedicated crop chemical player.

Markets, however, reacted sharply to the news. Corteva’s (CTVA) shares dropped 10% following the announcement, wiping out $4.5 billion in value in a broadly flat market. Including losses after media reports surfaced earlier in September, Zekauskas estimated the separation may have erased closer to $7.5 billion in market capitalization.

Still, J.P. Morgan sees the sell-off as excessive. Based on 2025 estimates, the seed business is valued at about 13.5 times ebitda, while the crop chemicals business trades at 6 times ebitda. On 2026 forecasts, the crop chemical arm is valued closer to 4x, a discount to rival FMC (FMC), which trades at 7.5 to 8 times despite weaker long-term growth prospects.

“A more normal trading multiple for Corteva’s crop chemical ebitda would be closer to nine to 10 times, or at a premium to FMC,” Zekauskas wrote.

The firm left its price target unchanged at $70, saying the sharp decline has created “an undervaluation of the company” rather than a true destruction of value.

data.title: The bond market drawdown just hit 62 months
data.description:
The U.S. bond market (NASDAQ:BND) is currently on the longest drawdown in history.

At 62 months and counting, U.S. bonds are experiencing its longest drawdown in history – since 1976 – with a maximum monthly drawdown of -17.2%. This current drawdown started in September 2020 and is ongoing.

The second-longest drawdown period lasted from July 1980 through October 1981 – 16 months – with a maximum monthly drawdown of -9%.

Here is a breakdown of the other periods:

 

 

 

“For decades, bonds were seen as a stabilizing force in portfolios, offering reliable income and price appreciation during periods of economic stress. That assumption has collapsed. The current bond market drawdown is not the result of a typical rate cycle. It reflects a repricing of sovereign risk in a world where inflation, fiscal dominance, and geopolitical fragmentation are all colliding,” according to EndGame Macro strategy, on X.com.

Investors can follow bond ETFs on Seeking Alpha’s bond page, here.

data.title: Perplexity now offers its AI-powered search engine Comet for free in challenge to Google
data.description:
The artificial intelligence startup Perplexity announced it is making Comet, its AI-powered search engine, free for all users worldwide.

The answer engine previously came with a hefty $200 per month subscription price. 

"We want to build a better internet, and that needs to be accessible to everybody," Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas said to Business Insider during an event in San Francisco on Wednesday.

The free version does come with rate limits, the report said.

The AI-powered search engine uses large language models to process queries, locate information and generate detailed responses from cited online sources.

Google Chrome continues to dominate the global search engine market share at 71% according to the latest estimates. Apple (AAPL) Safari and Microsoft (MSFT) Bing round out the top three.

On the same day, Perplexity also launched Comet Plus, which is designed to "deliver premium journalism to a more curious, empowered internet and ensure publishers benefit from the new demands of the AI age."

A standalone subscription for the service is $5 per month. Some of its partners include CNN, Conde Nast, Fortune, Le Figaro, Le Monde, The Los Angeles Times and The Washington Post.

Last month, Perplexity finalized a funding round that values the company at $20B after securing approximately $200M in new capital.

data.title: Algoma Steel slides to new all-time low after warning of up to C$90M quarterly loss
data.description:
Algoma Steel (NASDAQ:ASTL) -6.7% to the lowest since since it returned to public markets in 2021 after warning that it expects to post a Q3 earnings loss as steel shipments decline.

Algoma (NASDAQ:ASTL) said it expects to lose C$80 million-C$90 million in adjusted EBITDA for the quarter while shipments are guided at 415,000-420,000 net tons, compared to EBITDA of $3.5 million and shipments of 520,443 net tons during the same period last year.

The company has been struggling since President Trump imposed 50% import tariffs on steel earlier this year, and the U.S. market accounted for ~60% of its business last year.

Algoma (ASTL) received C$500 million in government loans earlier this week to help offset the impacts of tariffs, but the shares have fallen for three straight sessions as the Canadian government's financial lifeline did little to ease investor concerns over the tariffs.

The loan money "will literally go up in flames if you don't change the trade policy for offshore imports or find a solution with the U.S.," Barry Zekelman, CEO of Zekelman Industries, told Bloomberg; his firm owns a 3% stake in Algoma (ASTL).

"The operating environment currently remains very challenging, and earnings weakness is expected to persist," BMO steel analyst Katja Jancic said in a note.

data.title: AbbVie and Calico win FDA fast track tag for kidney drug
data.description:
Calico Life Sciences announced on Thursday that the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) has issued Fast Track Designation for ABBV-CLS-628, a kidney disease therapy developed by the company in partnership with AbbVie (NYSE:ABBV).

The treatment targeting autosomal dominant polycystic kidney disease is currently undergoing a Phase 2 trial for the indication involving nearly 95 trial sites globally, the San Francisco, California-based biotech said.

Characterized by the formation of fluid-filled cysts in the kidneys, ADPKD is the most common genetically developed kidney disease in the world. More than 50% of patients with ADPKD can develop kidney failure before they reach 60 years, requiring dialysis or a transplant as the only available treatment options.

The FDA fast-track designation allows a faster timeline for the development and review of drugs for serious conditions with unmet medical needs. Subject to certain conditions, companies with fast-track designations can secure the FDA's accelerated approval and priority review to bring their products to market sooner.
